---
title: "CodeBook"
date: "2024"
date-format: "YYYY"
image: "../images/CodeBook.png"
# codelink: "https://github.com/LorcanMcLaren/"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: article
    template-partials:
      - "../partials/title-block.html"
---

CodeBook is a codebook-driven text annotation tool for computational social science. A single JSON codebook file defines your annotation task for human annotators, serves as input to an LLM annotation pipeline, and can be exported as a LaTeX or Markdown document to render as a PDF or paper appendix.

Creating large sets of annotated text is a common task in many text-as-data approaches. Recent advancements in LLMs have accelerated this trend, as domain-specific ground truth is important for validating zero- and few-shot models, or for fine-tuning.

CodeBook provides the following features:

- Consistent experience for teams of annotators
- Low-latency, reproducible annotation workflows
- Multiple annotation types: binary, dropdown [categorical], Likert scale, and open ended-text
- Fully customisable codebook editor with integrated instructions and examples
- Codebook export in LaTeX and Markdown formats for easy rendering in papers and appendices
- LLM prompt preview and export for pipeline integration
- CSV input and output for interoperability

Try the tool [here](https://codebook.streamlit.app/). Feel free to reach out if you encounter any bugs or need additional features for your annotation task.
